steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a modifier and sentence continuity question. The opening sentence establishes Rabinal Achí as the subject. The blank follows the participial phrase “Based on events that occurred when Rabinal was a city-state ruled by a king,” which must modify the subject of the independent clause that follows. The decisive clue: the dance drama — not the military leader K’iche’ Achí — must be the subject, since the participial phrase describes the drama’s basis, and the sentence must then continue that drama as its grammatical focus.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Rabinal Achí tells the story of K’iche’ Achí, a military leader who’: Rabinal Achí is the subject, correctly modified by the opening participial phrase; the relative clause “who had once been an ally…” then provides information about K’iche’ Achí within a grammatical structure. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘K’iche’ Achí, the military leader in the story of Rabinal Achí,’: the subject is now K’iche’ Achí, causing the participial phrase to dangle — the story, not the character, is based on those historical events. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘the military leader whose story is told in Rabinal Achí, K’iche’ Achí,’: the subject is again the military leader, producing the same dangling modifier error; the appositional structure also leaves the main clause without a clear predicate for the blank. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘there was a military leader, K’iche’ Achí, who in Rabinal Achí’: the existential subject “there” causes the participial phrase to dangle; placing “in Rabinal Achí” between the relative pronoun “who” and the verb also violates standard relative clause word order. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
